Do You Experience This?

Signs You Need an Eye Test

Headaches

Frequent headaches can signal uncorrected vision problems

Blurry Vision

Difficulty reading or seeing objects at any distance

Eye Fatigue

Tired or strained eyes after reading or screen use

Screen Glare

Digital eye strain from extended computer or phone use

Night Vision

Struggling to see clearly in low-light conditions

Latest Articles

Eye Care Tips & Advice

Expert eye health advice from our team at Dynamicspecs Optometrists, Vanderbijlpark.

Eye Health
15 January 2026

How Often Should You Have an Eye Test?

Many people only visit an optometrist when they notice a problem with their vision — but by then, early-stage conditions may have already progressed. Regular eye examinations are one of the most important things you can do for your long-term health.

For Adults: A comprehensive eye exam is recommended every two years for healthy adults with no vision correction needs. If you already wear glasses or contact lenses, an annual exam is strongly advised to ensure your prescription remains accurate and your eyes stay healthy.

For Children: Children should have their first eye test before starting school, typically around age 5 or 6. Poor vision in children is often mistaken for learning difficulties or behavioural problems. Many schools also now require a vision screening certificate before enrolment.

For Seniors: Adults over 60 should have annual eye exams. The risk of cataracts, glaucoma, macular degeneration, and diabetic eye disease increases significantly with age — and most of these conditions are treatable when caught early.

At Dynamicspecs, our HPCSA-registered optometrist performs thorough examinations using professional equipment to assess not just your vision, but the overall health of your eyes.

Are you due for an eye test? Most people cannot tell when their prescription has changed — only a professional exam will confirm it. When last did you have yours checked?

Learn More About Our Eye Tests →
Digital Wellness
12 February 2026

Protecting Your Eyes from Blue Light

In today's world, most of us spend 8 or more hours a day staring at screens — phones, laptops, tablets, and TVs. While screens have become essential to work and life, prolonged exposure to the blue light they emit is having a serious impact on our eye health.

What is Blue Light? Blue light is a high-energy, short-wavelength light emitted by digital screens and LED lighting. Unlike other colours of light, blue light penetrates all the way to the back of the eye (retina) and over time can contribute to eye strain, headaches, dry eyes, and disrupted sleep.

Digital Eye Strain Symptoms: Blurry vision after screen use, headaches, burning or tired eyes, difficulty focusing, and neck or shoulder tension are all common signs of digital eye strain — a condition affecting millions of people who work with screens daily.

The Solution — Blue Light Filter Lenses: Blue light filter lenses have a special coating that reflects and absorbs blue light before it reaches your eyes. Studies show they significantly reduce the symptoms of digital eye strain, improve sleep quality, and provide long-term protection for your retina.

At Dynamicspecs, we offer blue light filter lenses for both prescription and non-prescription spectacles. Whether you're a student, office worker, or gamer — these lenses are one of the best investments you can make for your eye health.

Do your eyes feel tired or sore after using your phone or computer? This is a clear sign of digital eye strain. Could blue light filter lenses be the solution you need?

Learn More About Blue Light Lenses →
Dry Eye
8 March 2026

Understanding Dry Eye Syndrome

Dry eye syndrome is one of the most common eye conditions seen by optometrists, yet it is frequently under-diagnosed and poorly managed. If your eyes feel gritty, burn, or water excessively — you may be suffering from dry eye.

What Causes Dry Eye? Dry eye occurs when your eyes either do not produce enough tears, or the tears evaporate too quickly. Contributing factors include prolonged screen use, air conditioning, contact lens wear, certain medications, hormonal changes (especially in women over 40), and ageing.

Common Symptoms:

  • Burning or stinging sensation in the eyes
  • Redness and irritation
  • Blurry vision that improves after blinking
  • Excessive tearing (the eye's response to dryness)
  • Sensitivity to light
  • Discomfort when wearing contact lenses

Treatment Options: Dry eye is very treatable. Depending on the cause and severity, treatment may include lubricating eye drops, lifestyle adjustments (such as screen breaks and humidifiers), dietary changes (omega-3 fatty acids), or prescription therapy. Our optometrist will assess your specific situation and recommend the most effective treatment plan.

Don't ignore dry eye — left untreated, it can lead to corneal damage and permanent vision problems.

Do your eyes feel constantly irritated, gritty, or red? These are the hallmark signs of dry eye syndrome. Are you getting the right treatment, or just managing the symptoms?

Learn More About Dry Eye Treatment →
Children's Vision
10 April 2026

Children's Eye Health — What Parents Should Know

A child's vision plays a critical role in their learning, development, and quality of life. Yet many vision problems in children go undetected for years simply because children cannot always articulate what they are experiencing — or they assume everyone sees the world the way they do.

Warning Signs in Children:

  • Sitting very close to the TV or screen
  • Squinting or tilting the head to see better
  • Rubbing eyes frequently
  • Complaining of headaches after reading or school
  • Losing their place while reading
  • Poor hand-eye coordination or clumsiness
  • One eye turning in or out

Vision Screening Certificates for School: Many South African schools require a vision screening certificate before Grade 1 entry. At Dynamicspecs, we provide same-day vision screening certificates for school admission, employment, and drivers' licence renewal — performed by our HPCSA-registered optometrist.

When Should Children Have Eye Tests? We recommend a first eye exam at age 3-4, before starting school, and then annually through the school years. Early detection of conditions like amblyopia (lazy eye), strabismus (squint), and myopia (short-sightedness) is essential — many of these conditions are only fully correctable when treated in childhood.

Bring your child to Dynamicspecs — we make eye tests fun, gentle, and stress-free for young patients.

When last did your child have a professional eye test? If they are struggling at school or showing any of the above signs, their vision may be the cause. Could undetected vision problems be holding your child back?

Book a Children's Eye Test →
🔴
Contact Lenses
Dynamicspecs Guide

Contact Lenses vs Glasses — Which Is Right for You?

This is one of the most common questions we receive at Dynamicspecs — and the honest answer is that both options have real advantages. The right choice depends on your lifestyle, prescription, eye health, and personal preference.

Advantages of Glasses: Glasses are easier to maintain, require no touching of the eye, can be a fashion accessory, and are often more affordable in the long run. Modern frames are lightweight and stylish — at Dynamicspecs we stock designer brands including FUBU, Felicia, D&G, Prada, Gucci, and Burberry.

Advantages of Contact Lenses: Contacts provide a wider field of vision, are ideal for sport and outdoor activities, and are not affected by weather (no fogging). Many people feel more confident without visible eyewear.

Things to Consider: Contact lenses require more care and hygiene discipline. They are not suitable for all prescriptions or all eye conditions. People with dry eye syndrome, for example, often find contact lenses uncomfortable. Our optometrist will assess whether contact lenses are appropriate for your eyes before fitting.

Many of our patients actually use both — glasses at home and contacts for social occasions or sport. This gives you the best of both worlds.

Not sure whether glasses or contact lenses suit your lifestyle better? Our optometrist can help you make the right decision based on your prescription, eye health, and daily routine. Would you like a professional recommendation?

📋
Certificates
Dynamicspecs Guide

Vision Screening Certificates Explained

A vision screening certificate is an official document issued by a registered optometrist confirming that your eyesight meets the required standard for a specific purpose. In South Africa, these certificates are commonly required for drivers' licence renewal, certain employment positions, and school entry.

Drivers' Licence Vision Screening: The South African Road Traffic Act requires all drivers to have adequate vision. When renewing your drivers' licence, you must pass a vision test and obtain a certificate from an HPCSA-registered optometrist. At Dynamicspecs, we provide same-day certificates with no waiting — walk in and walk out.

School Entry Certificates: Many schools in Gauteng require proof of a vision screening before Grade 1 enrolment. This test checks that your child's vision meets the minimum standard required for classroom learning. We make this quick and child-friendly.

Occupational / Employment Certificates: Certain occupations — including security, transport, mining, and construction — require employees to have regular vision screenings. We provide professional certificates accepted by employers and licensing authorities.

You do not need a prior appointment for a vision screening certificate at Dynamicspecs — simply visit us at Shop 50, 1st Floor, Vaal Gate during trading hours.

Do you need a vision screening certificate for your drivers' licence, school, or employer? We issue same-day certificates at Dynamicspecs. No appointment necessary — but do you know exactly which certificate you need?
